observe: Too few frames, and a process stops running and starts just paging things in and out. That's not progress — that's thrashing.
fault: CPU utilization just dropped even though you added more work. That's the tower shaking — thrashing, not overload.
repair: Ease off. Fewer processes, more frames each — watch it stabilize.
escape: Feel that shake? Don't let it collapse. Reduce the load, now.

observe: Thrashing occurs when excessive paging consumes more time than execution.
fault: CPU utilization dropped despite increased process count, indicating thrashing.
repair: Reduce the degree of multiprogramming to relieve paging pressure.
escape: Confirm the thrashing condition is resolved.

hints: Watch the relationship between utilization and process count — thrashing inverts the expected trend.
